definition: A title used for the Buddha, often carrying doctrinal weight that should not be flattened into a casual English equivalent.
notes: The project leaves Tathāgata untranslated because attempts to fully render it in English are unstable and usually distract from the doctrinal use of the title. A short gloss is acceptable on first occurrence when needed.

context_rules.context: In most source-facing and doctrinal contexts
context_rules.notes: Default project practice is to leave the title untranslated.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a gloss is necessary
context_rules.notes: Use sparingly and only as a gloss.

example_phrases.notes: Common titular usage throughout the Nikāyas.
